pattern: Track how many partial selections end at each possible running gcd, folding one row at a time so the count of coprime full selections is just the tally at gcd 1.
hints: You pick one value per row; what matters about a partial pick is only the gcd accumulated so far.
Carry a table from gcd value to the number of ways to reach it; values are small so the table stays tiny.
For each new row, combine every current gcd with every value in the row via gcd, summing counts modulo 1e9+7; the answer is the entry for gcd 1.

edge_inputs.description: a one in any row guarantees coprimeness
all even values can never be coprime

stress.description: random small-value matrices where coprime picks occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 8)))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 8)))
    mat = [[rng.randint(1, 30) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [mat]
